title: Device and process for preparing spray dried powders
abstract: The invention relates to a device and a process for preparing a spray-dried powders. The device comprises a drying chamber defining a top end and an opposing bottom end; a high pressure nozzle located at about the top end of the drying chamber, the high pressure nozzle being adapted to supply an atomized stream of feed liquid and primary drying gas into the drying chamber; an outlet located at about the bottom end, the outlet being adapted to withdraw spray dried powders thus formed in the drying chamber; a size-based classification unit adapted to receive the spray dried powders and classify the same into a first class of spray dried powders and at least one further class of spray dried powders; and a low pressure nozzle located at about the top end of the drying chamber, the low pressure nozzle being adapted to receive the at least one further class of spray dried powders, a stream of steam and optionally a secondary drying gas and supply the same to the drying chamber.
claims: Claims CLAIMS: 1. A device for preparing a spray-dried powders, comprising: a drying chamber defining a top end and an opposing bottom end; a high pressure nozzle located at about the top end of the drying chamber, the high pressure nozzle being adapted to supply an atomized stream of feed liquid and primary drying gas into the drying chamber; an outlet located at about the bottom end, the outlet being adapted to withdraw spray dried powders thus formed in the drying chamber; a size-based classification unit adapted to receive the spray dried powders and classify the same into a first class of spray dried powders and at least one further class of spray dried powders; and a low pressure nozzle located at about the top end of the drying chamber, the low pressure nozzle being adapted to receive the at least one further class of spray dried powders, a stream of steam and optionally a secondary drying gas and supply the same to the drying chamber. 2. The device as claimed in claim 1, further comprising a grinding unit for grinding the at least one further class of spray dried powders prior to feeding the same to the low pressure nozzle. 3. The device as claimed in any of the preceding claims, further comprising a transporting unit adapted to transport the at least one further class of spray dried powders to the grinding unit or the low pressure nozzle. 4. The device as claimed in any of the preceding claims, wherein the high pressure nozzle and the low pressure nozzle are separated by a distance greater than 10% of the diameter of the drying chamber. 5. The device as claimed in any of the preceding claims, wherein the drying chamber is provided with an expansion zone at about an intermediate location and at least one gas withdrawing means at the expansion zone for withdrawing exhaust gas there-from. 6. The device as claimed in claim 5, wherein the gas withdrawal means is operably coupled to a solid-gas separator for separating solids present in the exhaust gas. 7. The device as claimed in claim 6, wherein the solid-gas separator is operably coupled to the transporting unit so as to feed the solid separated from the exhaust gas to the grinding unit or the low pressure nozzle. 8. A method for preparing a spray-dried powders, comprising: providing a drying chamber defining a top end and an opposing bottom end; supplying, by a high pressure nozzle located at about the top end of the drying chamber, an atomized stream of feed liquid and primary drying gas into the drying chamber; withdrawing, by an outlet located at about the bottom end of the drying chamber, spray dried powders thus formed in the drying chamber; classifying, by a size-based classification unit, the spray dried powders into a first class of spray dried powders and at least one further class of spray dried powders; receiving, by a low pressure nozzle located at about the top end of the drying chamber, the at least one further class of spray dried powders, a stream of steam and optionally a secondary drying gas; and supplying, by the low pressure nozzle, the at least one further class of spray dried powders, a stream of steam and optionally a secondary drying gas to the drying chamber. 9. The method as claimed in claim 8, further comprising a grinding, by a grinding unit, the at least one further class of spray dried powders prior to feeding the same to the low pressure nozzle. 10. The method as claimed in claim 8, further comprising: withdrawing, exhaust gas from the drying chamber; separating solids present in the exhaust gas; and transporting the solids thus separated to the low pressure nozzle for feeing to the drying chamber.
